title: Authorise access to the configured private artifact repository
objective: Allow authenticated users into the application only when they are authorised to access the configured private artifact repository through the installed GitHub App.
userContext: Signing into GitHub proves identity but does not by itself grant access to the private artifact library. Access should depend on both the GitHub App installation permissions and the signed-in user's access to the exact configured repository.
currentBehaviour: AUTH-001 provides authenticated sessions, but there is no repository-specific authorisation check, GitHub App installation verification or repository allowlist.
requiredBehaviour: After GitHub authentication, the application must verify that the configured private repository is accessible to both the installed GitHub App and the signed-in user. Users who fail this check must be denied access to artifact content.
userExperience: An authorised user enters the library normally. A signed-in but unauthorised user sees a clear access-denied page explaining whether repository access, app installation or configuration is missing, without exposing private artifact data.
functionalRequirements: Define configuration for the allowed repository owner and name.
Verify the GitHub App is installed with access to the configured repository.
Verify the signed-in GitHub user has access to the configured repository.
Ensure access is limited to the exact configured repository.
Deny application access when either the app or the user lacks repository access.
Recheck authorisation when a new session starts.
Handle revoked repository access or uninstalled GitHub App access.
Provide a clear unauthorised state distinct from unauthenticated state.
Support an optional allowlist of GitHub logins as defence in depth.
Protect all artifact pages and APIs with both authentication and repository authorisation.
Do not leak repository existence or content beyond what is necessary for the access-denied message.
technicalConsiderations: Prefer GitHub App user access tokens where access is limited by both user and installation permissions.
Keep user authentication, repository authorisation and repository data access as separate concerns.
Cache authorisation briefly where appropriate, but do not keep stale access indefinitely.
Do not use a broad personal access token.
acceptanceCriteria: A signed-in user without repository access is denied.
A user with repository access and a valid app installation is authorised.
Removing user repository access prevents subsequent authorised sessions.
Removing the GitHub App installation prevents access.
Only the configured repository is accepted.
Optional GitHub login allowlisting works when configured.
Authentication and authorisation failures are clearly distinguished.
Protected pages and APIs enforce the repository authorisation check.
No private artifact data is returned to unauthorised users.
Relevant tests and Cloudflare production build validation pass.
The current application specification is updated to describe repository-based authorisation.
The implementation pull request includes AUTH-002 and closes the generated issue.
codexGuidance: Build on AUTH-001. Do not implement artifact repository reading yet. Prefer an explicit authorisation service or policy function that later data and edit APIs can reuse.
